I can't afford it means that maybe I'm not as liquid as I like to be right now. But when someone becomes really, really fucking certain, you know what, but I'll stretch and buy it. I'm not liquid. I don't care about that one bit. Okay, 98% of the time it's just not true. Not that they're lying to you, but what they're saying is I'm not as liquid as I'd like to be. And when you make them more certain, the liquidity drops away. The point is, though, is that my tonality is slightly different with that objection. It's like, you know, I understand that, John, totally get that. Let me ask you a question. Get it. And with that, we'll end the formal part of the issue. It was a really important thing. Remember your anchors guy and let us start the Q&A. Awesome. Awesome. Great. Great training. The first one's from Jorge. He says, What do you do once you close? What do you do once you close to make sure you don't create a buyer's remorse, create buyers remorse and keep tight rapport. Great question. So number one, couple things. Buyers remorse, the main source of buyers remorse is when someone buys something, and you haven't really made that full scream from the hilltops airtight logical case, and you had someone that would be had a sort of a lower action threshold. See, people with high action thresholds, don't get buyers remorse, you know why? Because they don't fucking buy unless they're damn certain. Seriously, it's more people like me who get buyers remorse. You just say, I'm easy to sell to them. I say, What did I just do? I don't really need this thing. I don't really want that kind of piece of shit anywhere. I don't know. Is it really worth it? Did I really do the right thing? That's buyers remorse. So what what it really means is that the person doesn't really understand logically why they made the decision. So they feel bad about it. They were emotionally impulse. So the way you overcome the way you combat that one way for sure is to make sure that you make an airtight logical case and you reinforce it as you loop. That's number one. In terms of other things you could do, believe it or not, ready? The best way. Ask referrals. If you get someone to give you a referral on the spot, the best time to ask referrals is right there on the spot in the first call. Once someone gives you a referral, they're not going to have buyers remorse. Because now they've already told their friends how can they they even start giving you names. Right. Then how could they change their mind? Oh, I thought I fucked my they're almost becoming consistent with their actions. That's another way. Then there's all these other strategies, which are called stick strategies. And there's all this is a whole different topic. It's a training into itself here. But there are all these different ways where follow up letters, how your product is packaged. If you're a physical product, perfect example is Apple. Right. Have you ever bought something from Apple like an iPhone? When you open up that box, it's like, whoa, fucking, it was care of this gleaming and shiny and like, whoa, it's beautiful, right? Versus from a different company, you open the box cracks in the middle. And the thing is, you know, like what the fuck and you know, you get it. So that's every so all those things, all how the product looks when you ship it, making a follow up call two days later, just making sure you got the prior things going, okay, lots of different ways. But there's a whole training of stick tragedy. I'll do another time. All right, next question is from Bianca Cobb. How do you use the system to sell speaking events? This is the back half right all right, so The first they say let me think about for the first one you will loop back into presentation mode right for the banners answer It's just looks man All right, you look back you represent You build certainty for all three tens you ask for the order now a second time So you ask for the first time when you had your main presentation You ask for the other first time that they give you a objection you loop back you deflect you loop back Now you get hit with another objection and say now let me call you back Now you can't just imagine it will be like if you said them I understand you want to call me back But let me ask you like you can't do it again right you can only deflect once right so The way you handle this is you answer the objection you rebut the objection head on You answer it now in the strat now. This is 50 answers to every single objection so but you know There's also an SLP there's a lot now there's 50 of each one There's not specific to that but the point is is that let's say is let me let me call you back I say John I understand you want to call me back, but let me say this I've been doing this for a long time now John if there's one thing I know is that you know You know you say you want to call back more thing about you'll put this in the back and why you're a busy person I'm a busy person.
